title: Belia Darzu

{{Quote|Maggot of metal, rust, and rot. Sith life draws breath, old life does not.|Incantation of ''mechu-deru vitae'', a technique used by Belia Darzu to create technobeasts|<ref name="NEGD" />}}
'''Belia Darzu''' was a [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Shi'ido]] who reigned as a [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] during the last quarter of the [[New Sith Wars]]. A born [[Changeling/Legends|shape-shifter]], Darzu typically appeared as a tall [[Human/Legends|Human]] woman with short brown [[Hair/Legends|hair]]. In [[1250 BBY]], she led the [[New Sith|revived Sith Empire]] at the beginning of a period called the [[Sictis Wars]]. An accomplished [[Sith alchemist/Legends|Sith alchemist]], [[Lord/Legends|Lord]] Darzu studied an obscure [[Force power/Legends|Force technique]] known as ''[[mechu-deru]]'', leading her to concoct a [[Nanogene spore|virus]] capable of transforming its victims into [[Mutant|mutated]] [[Cyborg/Legends|cyborgs]]—[[technobeast]]s. Using a hidden [[Belia Darzu's fortress|fortress]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Tython/Legends|Tython]], she created an [[Metanecrons|army]] of technobeasts in a campaign against the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and its [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] protectors. By [[1230 BBY]], the Dark Lord had fallen victim to a conspiracy to [[Death/Legends|end]] her life. Pursued by her traitorous [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] followers and the vengeful [[Mecrosa Order]], Darzu's reign culminated in her [[Assassination of Belia Darzu|assassination]].

==Biography==
===Sictis Wars===
{{Quote|Do you know the name of Belia Darzu? She was a Dark Lord of the Sith who reigned over two centuries ago.|[[Hetton]]|<ref name="DBROT" />}}
[[File:Belia_Darzu.jpg|thumb|left|150px|Darzu demonstrating her shape-shifting ability.]]

Belia Darzu was a [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Shi'ido]] who lived during the [[New Sith Wars]],<ref name="END" /> a [[Galaxy/Legends|galactic]] conflict initiated in [[2000 BBY/Legends|2000 BBY]] by the [[Sith/Legends|Sith Order]], a [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] cult that opposed the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]].<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> A natural-born [[Changeling/Legends|shape-shifter]] like all members of her [[Species/Legends|species]], Darzu commonly assumed the physical form of a [[Human/Legends|Human]] woman.<ref name="DBROT" /> By [[1250 BBY]],<ref name="TEA" /> she had seized the mantle of [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]], rising to power over the [[New Sith|resurgent Sith empire]] at the beginning of a period called the [[Sictis Wars]].<ref name="END" /><ref name="TEA" /> Around this time, she researched [[Sith alchemy/Legends|Sith alchemy]] along with the principles of ''[[mechu-deru]]'', a [[Force power/Legends|Force technique]] granting absolute control over mechanical systems.<ref name="NEGD" />

At some point before the end of [[1230 BBY]],<ref name="Creation dates">''[[The New Essential Guide to Droids]]'' confirms [[1230 BBY]] as the date of Belia Darzu's death. Concurrently, ''[[Darth Bane: Rule of Two]]'' establishes Darzu as the creator of her holocron, while {{CSWECite|II|348|''Mystic''}} confirms Darzu as the creator of her fortress. Finally, ''[[The Dark Side Sourcebook]]'' cites Darzu as the creator her dark armor suit. It can be assumed that these possessions were created before the end of 1230 BBY at the very latest.</ref> Darzu built a secret [[Belia Darzu's fortress|stronghold]] on the [[Deep Core/Legends|Deep Core]] [[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[Tython/Legends|Tython]].<ref name="CSWE-MYSTIC">{{CSWECite|II|348|''Mystic''}}</ref><ref name="DBROT" /> At another time prior to the end of this date,<ref name="Creation dates" /> the Shi'ido also created a [[Belia Darzu's dark armor|personal battle armor suit]],<ref name="DSSB">''[[The Dark Side Sourcebook]]''</ref> as well as a [[Holocron of Belia Darzu|Sith holocron]]; Darzu's holocron—of which remained inside the underground inner sanctum of her fortress<ref name="DBROT" />—would eventually contain her accumulated knowledge of ''mechu-deru'' and Sith alchemy,<ref name="CSWE-DARZU" /> along with her philosophical views on [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] and instructions detailing the creation of a holocron.<ref name="DBROT" />

Harnessing the dark side energy spread throughout the planet Tython,<ref name="CSWE-TYTHON">{{CSWECite|III|273|Tython}}</ref> [[Lord/Legends|Lord]] Darzu used her fortress to research the creation of [[Cyborg/Legends|machine-organic]] hybrids known as [[technobeast]]s. In this pursuit, she studied the anatomy of several different species, documenting her work through a range of notes and sketches.<ref name="DBROT" /> With her knowledge of Sith alchemy and ''mechu-deru'',<ref name="NEGD" /> Darzu concocted the [[nanogene spore]],<ref name="CSWE-NANO">{{CSWECite|II|359|nanogene spore}}</ref> a [[Nanovirus|technovirus]] capable of growing metallic tumors on a victim's body. By means of a new incantation called ''[[mechu-deru vitae]]'', she used the nanogene spore to transform living beings into [[Sithspawn/Legends|Sithspawn]] [[mutant]]s—technobeasts.<ref name="NEGD" />

Using her stronghold to house the technobeasts,<ref name="DBROT" /> Darzu organized the creatures into an [[Army/Legends|army]] known as the [[Metanecrons]], forming the front line of her force against the Republic and the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] during the Sictis Wars.<ref name="END" /><ref name="TEA" /> Bound to Darzu's will,<ref name="DBROT" /> the technobeasts were capable of infecting other beings with the [[virus]],<ref name="NEGD" /> assimilating the Dark Lord's conquered foes into her [[Legion/Legends|legion]] of Sithspawn cyborgs.<ref name="END" /> Under Darzu's reign, the technobeasts came to be regarded as the defining horror of the Sictis Wars.<ref name="NEGD" />

By 1230 BBY,<ref name="NEGD-ROT">Logically, the alliance between Darzu and the Mecrosa must have fallen apart by 1230 BBY, the confirmed date of Darzu's death according to ''[[The New Essential Guide to Droids]]''.</ref> an alliance once established between Lord Darzu and the [[Mecrosa Order]]<ref name="DBROT" />—a secretive group of [[Assassin/Legends|assassins]] with former ties to the Sith<ref name="END" />—had fallen apart.<ref name="DBROT" /> The Mecrosa sought revenge against Darzu for an unwanted incursion in the [[Tapani sector/Legends|Tapani sector]], their territory.<ref name="END" /> The Dark Lord also faced betrayal from her own Sith followers, who somehow conspired in a plot to [[Death/Legends|end]] her life.<ref name="VADER">''[[Vader: The Ultimate Guide]]''</ref> The Mecrosa [[Assassination of Belia Darzu|fulfilled this plot]]—along with their revenge—by [[Poison/Legends|poisoning]] Darzu to death on the planet Tython.<ref name="END" /><ref name="NEGD" />

===Legacy===
{{Quote|I need a new Master—one with the power to penetrate the defenses of Belia's stronghold and lay claim to her secrets.|Hetton|<ref name="DBROT" />}}
Despite being deemed a Sith victory,<ref name="BS-SDS">''[[Book of Sith: Secrets from the Dark Side]]''</ref> the end of the Sictis Wars—concurrent with Darzu's death<ref name="NEGD" />—left no clear line of succession for another individual to seize the mantle of Dark Lord.<ref name="BS-SDS" />The fall of Darzu also halted the production of new technobeasts, though future Sith Lords continued to use them, albeit in smaller numbers, until the New Sith Wars finally ended in [[1000 BBY/Legends|1000 BBY]].<ref name="NEGD" /><ref name="TEA" /> Over two hundred years after the end of Darzu's reign, some of her technobeasts remained active, wandering throughout her stronghold on Tython without purpose or direction. The holocron of Lord Darzu—the last vestige of her power—radiated a dark side energy throughout the stronghold, drawing the remaining technobeasts to congregate around it. In [[990 BBY]], these technobeasts were destroyed by [[Darth Bane/Legends|Darth Bane]], a survivor of the New Sith Wars who [[Mission to Tython|ventured into]] Darzu's fortress, retrieved her holocron and ultimately uncovered the secrets to building such a device of his own.<ref name="DBROT" /> Centuries later, a number of [[Sith scroll/Legends|scrolls]] containing Darzu's secrets came into the possession of [[Roganda Ismaren/Legends|Roganda Ismaren]] and [[Cronal]], two [[Dark side adept/Legends|Dark Side Adepts]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]].<ref name="END" /> In the era of the [[Galaxy/Legends|galactic]] [[Yuuzhan Vong War]], the feats of Belia Darzu were briefly mentioned in [[Droids, Technology and the Force: A Clash of Phenomena|''Droids, Technology and the Force'']], an essay written by [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Tam Azur-Jamin]].<ref name="DTATF">{{Hyperspace|url=hyperspace/member/insideronline/81/indexp7.html|text=Droids, Technology and the Force: A Clash of Phenomena|int=Droids, Technology and the Force: A Clash of Phenomena (article)}}</ref>

==Personality and traits==
[[File:Beliadarzu.jpg|thumb|right|150px|Belia Darzu, a dark side marauder.]]

As a shape-shifter, Darzu commonly assumed the guise of a tall Human woman with short [[Color/Legends|brown]] [[Hair/Legends|hair]]. Appearing roughly thirty years old in this form, she possessed a sly, almost crafty look to her facial features. The avatar of Darzu's holocron—mimicking the Dark Lord's appearance and mannerisms—alternated through several different physical forms, even while in the middle of a conversation.<ref name="DBROT" />

==Powers and abilities==
{{Quote|She was a student of Sith alchemy; it was said she learned the secrets of mechu-deru, the ability to transform the flesh of living beings into metal and machinery.|Hetton|<ref name="DBROT" />}}
Belia Darzu was a [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] individual who became a [[dark side marauder]],<ref name="JCF-DSSB">{{JCFCite|url=posts/24350750|board=Literature|thread=Evil Never Dies: The Sith Dynasties|user=Halagad_Ventor|date=2006-07-11|time=5:15 PM|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20200801163409/https://boards.theforce.net/threads/evil-never-dies-the-sith-dynasties.24202765/page-14}}</ref> a role that combined physical prowess and the ability to wield the power of the Force for violent purposes.<ref name="DSSB" /> As a member of the Shi'ido race, Darzu was also capable of altering her physical form. This allowed her to mimic the appearance of the Human, [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]], [[Iridonian (species)|Iridonian]], or [[Cerean/Legends|Cerean]] species, as well as different genders. During her tenure as a Sith Lord, Darzu created a [[Sith holocron/Legends|Sith holocron]], instilling it with her own knowledge of the secrets to building such a device. This included the [[Rite of Commencement]], a long and complex ritual that trapped the cognitive essence of the creator into the [[capstone]] of the holocron.<ref name="DBROT" />

Skilled in the dark side arts of Sith alchemy and ''mechu-deru'',<ref name="CSWE-DARZU" /> Darzu used her knowledge of both to devise the nanogene spore, as well as the offshoot technique of ''mechu-deru vitae''. Developed from her own research, Darzu's technovirus consisted of tiny [[nanogene droid]]s. Upon skin contact with the victim, the virus grew metallic tumors over their bodies and ultimately [[Lobotomy|lobotomized]] their [[Brain/Legends|frontal lobes]], irreversibly transforming them into technobeasts.<ref name="NEGD" /> The technobeasts were bound to the will of Darzu, whose influence over the mutants compelled them to guard the Dark Lord's holocron even centuries after her death.<ref name="DBROT" />

==Equipment==
As a Sith Lord, Darzu wore a self-crafted suit of dark armor for optimal protection when engaging multiple [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] opponents.<ref name="NEGWT">''[[The New Essential Guide to Weapons and Technology]]''</ref> Imbued with the power of the dark side and worth 37,000 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]], this heavy plated [[battle armor]] bestowed enhanced physical strength upon its bearer, as well as stealth capabilities.<ref name="DSSB" /> In her typical Human appearance, Darzu garbed a dark, form fitting [[Flight suit/Legends|flight suit]] with a pale yellow vest that left her arms bare, save for a pair of short black sleeves covering her elbows. Complementing this outfit were a pair of dark [[Boot/Legends|boots]] and yellow [[Glove/Legends|gloves]], along with a red [[Hat/Legends|flight cap]] and a [[Belt/Legends|belt]].<ref name="DBROT" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:DarkSideEnforcerCard.jpg|thumb|left|150px|The Dark Side Enforcer figurine card, based on Belia Darzu.]]

Belia Darzu was first mentioned in ''[[The Dark Side Sourcebook]]'', a [[Roleplaying game|roleplaying]] sourcebook published in [[2001]] as a supplement for the first edition of ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''. In [[2006]], two images illustrated by [[Doug Alexander Gregory]]—one from ''The Dark Side Sourcebook'' showing a dark side marauder,<ref name="DSSB" /> and another from the ''[[Ultimate Alien Anthology]]'' featuring a Shi'ido changeling<ref name="UAA">''[[Ultimate Alien Anthology]]''</ref>—were [[Retroactive continuity|retconned]] by [[Abel G. Peña]] as depictions of Darzu's physical appearance, verifying the character's species.<ref name="JCF-DSSB" /><ref name="JCF-UAA">{{JCFCite|url=posts/24351423|board=Literature|thread=Evil Never Dies: The Sith Dynasties|user=Halagad_Ventor|date=2006-07-11|time=2:50 AM|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20200801163409/https://boards.theforce.net/threads/evil-never-dies-the-sith-dynasties.24202765/page-14}}</ref> Darzu's likeness was later used for the "Dark Side Enforcer" figurine from ''[[Star Wars Miniatures]]: [[Champions of the Force (Star Wars Miniatures)|Champions of the Force]]'', resembling the original dark side marauder.<ref name="COTF">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/championsgallery|text=''Champions of the Force'' Gallery}}</ref> In [[2007]], Darzu made her debut appearance (though only as a [[Gatekeeper/Legends|holocron gatekeeper]]) in ''[[Darth Bane: Rule of Two]]'', the sophomore novel of [[Drew Karpyshyn]]'s [[Star Wars: Darth Bane Trilogy|''Darth Bane Trilogy'']].<ref name="DBROT" /> The names of three demonic figures depicted in [[Wikipedia:John Milton|John Milton's]] [[Wikipedia:Paradise Lost|''Paradise Lost'']]—[[Wikipedia:Belial|Belial]], [[Wikipedia:Moloch|Moloch]] and [[Wikipedia:Mammon|Mammon]]—were used to identify the Shi'ido characters of "Belia Darzu", "[[Moloch Hoole]]" and "[[Mammon Hoole]]."<ref name="Lorrd">{{Blog|taloncard|12|Endnotes for "Death in the Slave Pits of Lorrd"}}</ref>

{{SuccessionBox
|title=[[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]]<ref name="DBROT" />
|years=Circa [[1250 BBY]]<ref name="TEA" />–[[1230 BBY]]<ref name="TEA" />
|before=[[Darth Rivan]]<ref name="END" /><ref name="EOJ">{{DoD|Echoes of the Jedi}}</ref>
|before-years=Sometime between 1417 BBY and [[1250 BBY]]<ref name="EOTJ-Rivan">According to ''[[Echoes of the Jedi]]'', [[Darth Rivan]] became a [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] sometime between 1,150 and 1,400 [[Standard year/Legends|years]] before the events of the story. It can be deduced that ''Echoes of the Jedi'' takes place in [[17 BBY/Legends|17 BBY]]; the story occurs two years after [[Order 66/Legends|Order 66]], which was declared in [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]] per ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''. As such, the earliest time of Rivan's reign can be dated at 1417 BBY. Furthermore, ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' confirms [[1250 BBY]] as the beginning date for the rule of Belia Darzu (established as Rivan's successor in ''[[Evil Never Dies: The Sith Dynasties]]''), thus providing the latest potential date for Rivan's reign.</ref>
|after=[[Skere Kaan]]<ref name="END" />
|after-years=[[1010 BBY]]–<ref name="TEA" />
}}
{{EndBox}}
